skill.name: gws-docs-write
skill.description: Append plain text to an existing Google Docs document with the gws command-line tool.
skill.summary: Append plain text to Google Docs from an AI coding assistant.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, relative links, and the word Description as dangerous patterns. Review found no malicious code, no prompt injection, and no real cryptographic use. The skill still carries medium risk because it instructs the assistant to run an external Google Workspace CLI command that writes to a Google Docs document.

medium_findings.title: External Google Docs Write Command
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run gws docs +write with a document ID and text. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can modify user documents and should require user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented command directly performs a Google Docs write action. The file also warns that confirmation is required, which reduces malicious intent concerns.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown inline code and fenced command examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable Ruby code or command substitution appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged backticks are Markdown formatting around text or examples. There is no source file that executes Ruby backticks.

low_findings.title: Relative Skill References Are Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The ../ paths point to sibling skill documentation files for shared Google Workspace guidance. They are documentation references, not file reads controlled by user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are static Markdown references to nearby skill files. No runtime filesystem API or user-controlled path construction is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the word Description in metadata and a Markdown table header. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only relevant text is ordinary documentation wording. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA-1, or any other weak cryptographic use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Document-Modifying CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The command pattern gws docs +write modifies a Google Docs document. It is expected behavior for this skill, but it is a write operation and must be confirmed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command name and surrounding caution text clearly describe a document append action. The pattern is risky because it changes remote user content.

content.value_statement: Teams often need to add AI-generated notes, summaries, or follow-ups to an existing Google document. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused Google Workspace command for appending plain text after user approval.
actual_capabilities: Append plain text to the end of an existing Google Docs document.
Use a document ID supplied by the user.
Pass user-provided text through the gws docs +write command.
Follow shared Google Workspace authentication and security guidance.
Warn the assistant to confirm before running the write command.
limitations: Requires the gws command-line tool and valid Google Workspace authentication.
Only appends plain text to the document body.
Does not create new Google Docs documents.
Does not apply rich formatting or complex layout changes.

use_cases.title: Append Meeting Notes
use_cases.description: Add approved meeting notes or action items to a shared Google document.

use_cases.title: Add Draft Content
use_cases.description: Send a reviewed paragraph, summary, or outline into an existing draft document.

use_cases.title: Update Team Logs
use_cases.description: Append status updates or operational notes to a running team log.

prompt_templates.title: Append Simple Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with a known document ID.
prompt_templates.prompt: Append this text to Google Doc DOC_ID: The meeting starts at 10:00 UTC.

prompt_templates.title: Append Reviewed Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Add content after the user approves the final wording.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these notes for clarity, then ask me to confirm before appending them to Google Doc DOC_ID.

prompt_templates.title: Append a Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Summarize source material before writing to a document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the discussion below in five bullets, then append the approved summary to Google Doc DOC_ID.

prompt_templates.title: Append Operational Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with explicit confirmation and audit context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a dated status update from this task report. Show the exact text first, then append it to Google Doc DOC_ID only after I approve.

output_examples.input: Append a short follow-up note to the project document.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares the note, asks for confirmation, then appends the approved plain text to the specified Google Doc.

output_examples.input: Add these action items to the team meeting notes.
output: Formats the action items as plain text.
Confirms the target document ID and final text.
Runs the Google Workspace write command after approval.

best_practices: Confirm the document ID and exact text before running the write command.
Use this skill for plain text updates that belong at the end of a document.
Keep a visible record of the text that was appended.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for sensitive content unless the user confirms the destination document.
Do not use this skill when rich formatting or tables are required.
Do not run the write command without explicit user approval.

faq.question: Does this skill create a new Google Doc?
faq.answer: No. It appends plain text to an existing document identified by document ID.

faq.question: Can it format headings, lists, or tables?
faq.answer: No. The skill is limited to plain text append operations.

faq.question: Does it require Google Workspace authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The gws command-line tool must be installed and authenticated.

faq.question: Should the assistant ask before writing?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly warns that write commands require user confirmation.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Where is the text inserted?
faq.answer: The text is inserted at the end of the Google Docs document body.
